I run this meeting and everyone waits for my call.
The decision is mine to make and I make it without hesitating.
I delegate the tasks and set the deadlines; the team falls in line.
I steer the conversation and the room follows my lead.
When I speak people quiet down to listen.
I decide where we are going tonight and that settles it.
The storm can do what it likes; I have already planned around it.
I do not wait on the forecast, I bend the day to my terms.
Rain or shine, the schedule answers to me.
I pick the restaurant and the table and nobody argues.
I order for the table because I know what is best here.
The kitchen runs on my timing tonight.
I go to bed when I decide the day is done, not before.
My evening runs on my schedule and answers to no one.
I set the alarm for when I choose to rise.
I set the pace and the others keep up or fall back.
I call the sets and the reps and we move on my count.
The court is mine the moment I step onto it.
I choose the route and we take it.
The itinerary is mine; everyone else just packs.
I tell the driver where to go and we go.
I read the headlines and decide what actually matters.
The world can churn; I set my own agenda regardless.
I judge the story on my terms, not the anchor's.
This is my house and it runs by my rules.
I decide what gets fixed first and when.
The thermostat, the table, the schedule are all my call.
